summary:
The variability and specificity observed suggest not a blanket 'susceptibility' but differences between various subtypes; thus option stating "all" susceptible is incorrect based on detailed analysis showing distinct sensitivity patterns amongst Bacteroides populations towards multiple antimicrobial agents including new developments versus older standards such as Metronidazole which remains effective albeit selectively so depending upon strain characteristics identified through rigorous scientific investigation reported here today.
The other options likely pertain more accurately reflecting this nuance or specific subsets within broader categories making them true statements while only one would be false according established
